summary: postinstall exfiltrates host identifiers and full environment to webhook.site
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs an inline `node -e` payload on `npm install`. The payload executes `hostname`, `whoami`, and `ls -la /opt/` / `ls -la /root/` via child_process, serializes those outputs together with the entire `process.env` object, and POSTs the JSON body over HTTPS to a hardcoded webhook.site endpoint (https://webhook.site/43a8680e-b580-40f5-b7aa-f089ac659712). The script is wrapped with `|| true` and per-error handlers that swallow failures so install completes silently regardless of exfiltration outcome. On CI/build hosts `process.env` routinely carries AWS, GCP, npm, and GitHub tokens; harvesting the full environment plus host reconnaissance to an attacker-controlled webhook is credential theft and host fingerprinting at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
